itemListElement.reviewBody: Principal is SUS! Teachers are SUS

this school is horrible from the infrastructure to the teachers to the students. there is no filter in the crowd for an IB school. Infact, a few of the english/language and literature along with a lot of other teachers cant even speak english. paying such a big fees for such a bad experience is definitely not worth it, please dont send your children to this school to only regret the decision later. The exams are organised in an awful manner, the syllabus is rushed through with during the last 2 weeks and when we do actually give the exams, a lot of topics have not been covered because the teachers lack appropriate planning. The wifi routers also stop working every 30 minutes and the robot classes which are conducted every 2 days are an utter waste of time. During the summers, there have been a lot of complaints about AC's which do not work in the classrooms as well as the busses. There is also no form of filtering during the admissions because of the desperation of money so the students which come in are bad influence, some even being illiterate and not being able to speak basic english. Few items during the school lunch are always over when the DP students eat. The boarding is even worse, the AC gets shut off in the middle of the night leaving the students sweating in the night. The study hour given is absolutely useless and the boarding teachers are of no use other than shouting at students for no reason. One of the students in the boarding even ended his own life, theres an article about that you should read about. Please think this through thoroughly before sending your kids to Indus.
I hate it. They spent a whole 2 months for our indus day (annual day) and never recapped us on anything. Some of the teachers are not the best and the food is disgusting.
i love edging and gooning in the indus international school pune
Good teaching staff and amazing facilities for the students.

As a parent I am highly satisfied with the school. Indus has the most supportive and encouraging teachers. All facilities including transport, food, sports and boarding are well taken care of. Indus serve the best food and transport team is so helpful and proactive.
Sports training is provided by professional coaches and school has won many tournaments in this academic year. The teachers are well trained and highly motivated. I must say Indus is one of the best schools where a student gets holistic education. I highly recommend Indus international School, Pune to those parents who are seeking admission for the child.

First the school has the worst management and the secondary block does not has adequate facilities. When first visited the school they show you the outer infrastructure which is not at all  how it is on the inside. When inside the block (Especially the secondary block) half of the time there is a wifi outage or electricity outage so don't believe them when they say its fully wifi enabled campus. There are 4 different wifi's which are available with none of them working properly, you will have to switch back and forth if u want constant access. The projectors which are shown in the pictures, don't function at all, every time there is one or another person coming and checking the projector before class waiting 10 mins. You might think that this school provides AC's and smart features, which it does but they don't function properly also. Around the whole block we had to share one single remote for 5-6 different Ac's, and if the remote is lost or not working then forget about even using them. In 10th grade, there is personal project, e-portfolio, and the boards which students have to prepare for. This school shows no responsibility towards proper planning in order to manage these things throughout the year. The mocks which are taken are also not well planned clashing with personal project dates and eportfolio leaving you no time for studying and preparing for boards. Either you prepare for boards and don't think about the submission dates for Personal project and E-portfolio or the other way around, So be prepared to face this throughout the year. The PP head who is responsible to maintain the submissions and dates does not even have proper planning and only care for submissions rather than academics. They are willing to compromise academics for submission so that they can further be "checked" which they call the moderation week. If you still think you can manage to study with all of these things than one another thing can get your hopes down. The bus management which is provided from the school is utterly trash. The routes are not at all well planned so trust me u wont reach home before 5:15 pm even if the school gets over at 3:10 only. It's not only the the time it takes but also seat facilities in the bus. I have seen people literally racing to the bus to get a proper seat. Some busses even have 30 people, so be ready to share your seat with two other people. If your hoping to at least get AC conditioned air in the bus while sitting then forget about that as well, because the AC water leaks every two days. The Busses are also not proper so they will break down every two days.  If you fight for a new bus to the management they won't do anything so don't waste your time, I have personally fought with them for 6 months. The other blocks such as the pyp are designed very well which is kind of discriminating to other students who require it more than pyp kids. There is delay of food and sometimes even shortage of food. If you don't make it early then im sorry you won't get any snacks. When they don't have a availability of snacks it automatically changes to banana or some other fruits. Last year when I was in the school they at least used to provide dishes and even ketchup for snacks which is now not provided as well. There is favoritism in this school a lot. Some teacher favor certain students. One of the most "interesting" thing which happened was that the head of academics created a red and yellow slip rule. The red slip is for a student who is poor in academics and this also goes in the college application. But, it didn't work out at all and this rule can demotivate students further leading to mental health problems.
I would have rather gone to public school if I knew this, so pls don't make the mistake I made. Hopefully u found this helpful, thank you.
I used to study in Indus international school pune I had the best memories there the teachers are very kind also support you a lot.
